title: armeria maritima subsp. maderensis
intro: Armeria maritima subsp. maderensis, also known as Madeiran thrift or Maderense Sea Thrift, is a subspecies of the Sea Thrift plant, a low-growing, evergreen perennial native to the Atlantic coast of Europe and North Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its essential oil and saponins. The health benefits of Armeria maritima subsp. maderensis include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its possible role in protecting against infections and promoting digestive health. Armeria maritima subsp. maderensis is typically prepared as a tea or infusion, and its essential oil can also be used in aromatherapy or as a topical treatment, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
